content: The Indus Valley Civilization is located in an area that includes Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Baluchistan, and extends to south India, north-eastern Afghanistan, and north-eastern Persia. The civilization's distribution covers a large area, approximately 1,000 miles from Rupar to Sutkagen-dor. Specific sites include Mohenjo-daro, the citadel-mound, and the mounds of Nindowari near the Kud river in the Ornach region, which is about 150 miles north-north-west of Karachi.

The Indus Valley Civilization is located near the right bank of the Kud river in the Ornach region, at a distance of 150 miles north-north-west of Karachi, and also in other cities such as Mohenjo-daro, and possibly Lahore or Multan.

The streets of Mohanjodaro were laid out in a grid pattern with main streets running north-south and east-west, dividing the area into blocks of roughly equal size and approximately rectangular shape, with the main streets being about 30 feet wide. The major insulae or blocks were subdivided by lanes which were not infrequently dog-legged, and these lanes were normally from 5 to 10 feet wide.
